sourceText: The most miserable of men is he who is deprived of the power of learning.
Knowledge is a fundamental human need, and its absence leads to true poverty.
I saw Aristotle in a dream, and he told me, 'The good is that which is good in the mind.'
The belief that true value and goodness are determined by reason and intellect, which encouraged him to seek out rationalist Greek philosophy.
Let the people be free in their beliefs.
This is somewhat ironic given the later 'Mihna', but it reflects his earlier, more tolerant stance and the general spirit of intellectual inquiry he fostered.

Though I gathered immense knowledge over decades of travel, my greatest pain is that most of my written works, including a 30-volume history, were lost to time. A significant portion of my life's work did not survive for posterity.

However, my surviving work, particularly 'The Meadows of Gold', established a new approach that integrated history, geography, and culture. This book alone secured my legacy, ensuring I would be known as the 'Herodotus of the Arabs' and continue to inspire scholars.
Only one who escapes the narrow sky inside a well and walks the world directly on two feet can gain true and three-dimensional knowledge.
It means the empirical attitude of recording vivid history and geography by directly exploring vast continents and oceans without relying solely on literature.
The breath of the times is contained in the lives and customs of all people, and history must remember the stories of the people more than the names of kings.
It refers to the insight that achieved broad academic accomplishments by deeply observing not only political events but also the daily lives and environments of diverse cultures.
True learning finally begins with an open mind that takes off the glasses of prejudice and respects the culture of an unfamiliar other.
